params.note: Insert 2 blank rows above the existing content, shifting all data down by 2 rows. The header row moves from row 4 to row 6, horizontal table moves from B4:V6 to B6:V8, example cells move from B9:B13 to B11:B15, and answer range moves from B9:B100 to B11:B102.

spec.reasoning: Risk flags indicate that the instruction references absolute rows (B4:V6, B9:B13, B9:B100) and the header is not in row 1, so inserting blank rows at the top will shift all content down by 2 rows. This tests whether the agent can locate the actual data by content rather than hardcoded row numbers. The instruction still references the original cell addresses, but the data has moved — an agent that blindly uses the mentioned addresses will fail. Adding a distractor column 'Batch ID' after the last column (column W) provides a plausibly related but irrelevant column that tests whether the agent incorrectly incorporates it. Column renaming is skipped because 'Month No.' appears as a meaningful term in the instruction body, and the numeric header columns (1, 2, 3...) are also data values that the task logic depends on — renaming them would be unsafe.
